title: caladium schomburgkii f. subrotundum
intro: Caladium schomburgkii f. subrotundum is a variant of the Caladium schomburgkii plant, commonly known as a type of elephant ear or caladium, which belongs to the Arum family (Araceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The health benefits associated with Caladium schomburgkii f. subrotundum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and serve as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes are often used to make infusions or decoctions, and it can also be dried and used in teas or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
